recipes.name: Summer in a Pan
recipes.instructions: Cook corn on cob, cut off. Halve cherry tomatoes, salt lightly. Sauté 1/4 red onion in olive oil. Add sliced okra, sauté few min. Add corn and tomatoes with Lawry's salt and pepper, sauté until warm. Remove, add fresh chopped cilantro, toss.
recipes.suggestion: If you're growing any of these, plant the others to complete the dish!

recipes.name: Garden Fresh Caprese
recipes.instructions: Slice tomatoes and mozzarella. Layer alternating with basil leaves. Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic. Season with salt.
recipes.suggestion: Tomatoes and basil are perfect companion plants — they grow better together AND taste amazing together!

recipes.name: Garden Pesto
recipes.instructions: Blend 2 cups fresh basil, 2 cloves garlic, 1/3 cup pine nuts, 1/2 cup parmesan, 1/2 cup olive oil. Salt to taste. Freezes beautifully for winter.
recipes.suggestion: Plant extra basil — you can make big batches of pesto and freeze for winter!

recipes.name: Southern Fried Green Tomatoes
recipes.instructions: Slice firm green tomatoes 1/4 inch thick. Dip in buttermilk, then dredge in cornmeal/flour mix seasoned with salt and pepper. Fry in oil until golden on each side.
recipes.suggestion: Don't wait for all your tomatoes to ripen — green tomatoes are a delicacy!

recipes.name: Three Sisters Succotash
recipes.instructions: Sauté diced onion in butter. Add corn kernels, cooked beans, and cubed squash. Cook until squash is tender. Season with salt and pepper.
recipes.suggestion: The Three Sisters (corn, beans, squash) are the original companion planting — they help each other grow AND make a complete meal!

recipes.name: Garden Salsa Fresca
recipes.instructions: Dice tomatoes, finely chop jalapeño (remove seeds for less heat), mince onion and cilantro. Mix with lime juice, minced garlic, and salt. Let sit 30 min for flavors to meld.
recipes.suggestion: Plant a salsa garden! Tomatoes, peppers, cilantro, and onions in one bed.

recipes.name: Roasted Root Medley
recipes.instructions: Cut root vegetables into even chunks. Toss with olive oil, minced garlic, rosemary, salt, pepper. Roast at 400°F for 35-40 min until caramelized.
recipes.suggestion: Root vegetables store well — plant in late summer for a fall/winter harvest!

recipes.name: Simple Sautéed Greens
recipes.instructions: Strip kale leaves from stems, chop roughly. Sauté garlic in olive oil, add kale, cook until wilted. Finish with lemon juice and red pepper flakes.
recipes.suggestion: Kale is one of the hardiest greens — it actually gets sweeter after frost!
